platform_note: Detailed prototype profile for NGAEX — Nigeria Raw Export Index Fund. Prepared for WASI Intelligence platform upload.

fund_family.shared_family_rule: Each country fund keeps its own export methodology while staying aligned to a continent-wide naming, governance, and packaging structure.
fund_family.comparison_currency_note: Publish the country in NGN and publish a continent-wide comparison series in USD.

investment_objective.primary_objective: Track the evolution of the main raw materials exported from Nigeria through a transparent, rules-based benchmark measured in NGN.
investment_objective.secondary_objective: Provide a future foundation for a regulated investment product linked to Nigeria's export economy and the broader AFEX continental architecture.
economic_thesis: Nigeria is Africa's largest oil producer and the continent's biggest economy, with an export base heavily concentrated in hydrocarbons.
Crude oil and natural gas account for over 90% of export revenues, creating a benchmark dominated by energy.
Agricultural exports (cocoa, sesame, rubber) represent a secondary but structurally important layer.
NGN denomination makes the benchmark locally relevant for Nigerian institutional investors and pension funds.
The Lagos-Apapa and Bonny Island export corridors anchor the physical flow of Nigerian commodity exports.

benchmark_methodology.weight_definition: Each constituent weight is the commodity's share of trailing twenty-year average export tonnage.
benchmark_methodology.transport_basis_note: Use outbound export tonnage from Bonny, Forcados, Escravos, Brass terminals and Lagos/Apapa port complex.
benchmark_methodology.calculation_logic: Index level changes according to weighted commodity return movements in NGN.
benchmark_methodology.coverage_rule: Include major raw materials exported from Nigeria, even where no liquid futures market exists.
benchmark_methodology.methodology_caution: Extreme energy concentration means the index is heavily correlated with global oil prices. Pure tonnage weighting by volume amplifies LNG and crude relative to high-value agricultural exports.

constituent_universe.name: Crude Oil
constituent_universe.export_note: Nigeria produces Bonny Light, Forcados, Qua Iboe, Escravos, and Brass River crudes. OPEC member.

constituent_universe.name: Natural Gas (LNG)
constituent_universe.export_note: Nigeria LNG (Bonny Island) is one of the world's largest LNG plants. Six-train facility.

constituent_universe.name: Cocoa Beans
constituent_universe.export_note: Nigeria is the world's fourth-largest cocoa producer. Southwest Nigeria is the main growing region.

constituent_universe.name: Sesame Seeds
constituent_universe.export_note: Nigeria is a top global sesame exporter. Mainly from Benue, Nasarawa, and Jigawa states.

constituent_universe.name: Natural Rubber
constituent_universe.export_note: Produced in Edo, Delta, and Ondo states. Nigeria was historically a major rubber producer.

constituent_universe.name: Urea (Fertilizer)
constituent_universe.export_note: Dangote Fertiliser plant (Lagos Free Trade Zone) — one of the largest urea plants in Africa.

illustrative_weight_note.explanation: Illustrative weights come from available trade data estimates and should be replaced with official twenty-year shipment data from NBS and Nigeria Customs before institutional launch.

country_context.corridor_relevance: Nigeria's port system (Lagos/Apapa, Onne, Calabar) serves as a major corridor for the entire West African subregion. The Lagos-Cotonou-Lome coastal artery and the northern routes to Niger and Chad are critical trade links.
country_context.special_note: Detailed prototype. Energy-dominant benchmark representing Africa's largest economy. Extreme oil/gas concentration is a feature, not a bug — it reflects the structural reality of Nigeria's export economy.

regulatory_positioning.securities_regulator: Securities and Exchange Commission Nigeria (SEC Nigeria)
regulatory_positioning.collective_investment_note: A future collective investment product or ETF would require SEC Nigeria approval and potentially NGX listing.
regulatory_positioning.product_separation_note: NGAEX Fund should be constituted and distributed separately from any microfinance or banking balance sheet.
regulatory_positioning.tax_considerations: Capital gains tax on securities, companies income tax, and VAT considerations under FIRS rules.
regulatory_positioning.pia_note: The Petroleum Industry Act 2021 reformed the oil and gas regulatory framework. NGAEX methodology must account for any impact on export volume reporting.
